description: Manufacturing Round 2 Run 4: APS Core Engine (4 scheduling strategies + Gantt) + MES Handlers + Quality Handlers

userStories.title: Create SchedulingStrategy interface + ForwardFifoStrategy
acceptanceCriteria: SchedulingStrategy interface and all DTOs exist in module/aps/
ForwardFifoStrategy implements SchedulingStrategy
FIFO sorts jobs by arrival time
Resource matching by type works correctly
Setup time added on product changes
Due date conflicts detected and reported
ForwardFifoStrategyTest has 5+ tests
Tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*ForwardFifoStrategyTest'
userStories.notes: TDD required. This is a large story but the DTOs + strategy are tightly coupled. The setup time matrix key format: "productId1-productId2" → minutes. When same product, setup time = 0.

userStories.title: Implement ForwardEddStrategy + BackwardStrategy
userStories.description:
Create two more scheduling strategies.

1. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/ForwardEddStrategy.java
@Component("forwardEdd")
Same as ForwardFifo but sorts jobs by due date ascending (Earliest Due Date first). Jobs with earlier deadlines get scheduled first.

2. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/BackwardStrategy.java
@Component("backward")
Schedules from due date backward:
- For each job, calculate latest start = dueDate - processingTime - setupTime
- If latest start < now → conflict (can't start in time)
- Assign to resources working backward from due date
- Resource availability tracked backward (latest occupied time)

Test: platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/SchedulingStrategiesTest.java

TDD:
- testEddOrdersByDueDate: job A due Apr 10, job B due Apr 5 → B scheduled first
- testEddSameResource: 2 jobs same resource type, earlier due date gets priority
- testBackwardFromDueDate: job due Apr 15, processing=60min → start Apr 14 23:00 (or within working hours)
- testBackwardConflict: job due tomorrow, processing=2 days → conflict reported
- testBackwardMultipleJobs: 3 jobs → all scheduled backward from their due dates
acceptanceCriteria: ForwardEddStrategy sorts by due date ascending
BackwardStrategy schedules from due date backward
BackwardStrategy reports conflict when start time is before now
SchedulingStrategiesTest has 5+ tests covering both strategies
Tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*SchedulingStrategiesTest'
userStories.notes: TDD required. Backward scheduling is conceptually different — instead of pushing jobs forward from now, it pulls them backward from due dates. The challenge is handling resource contention when multiple jobs need the same resource at overlapping backward-scheduled times.

userStories.title: Implement BottleneckFirstStrategy (Drum-Buffer-Rope)
userStories.description:
Create the PCBA-optimized bottleneck scheduling strategy.

1. platform/src/main/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/BottleneckFirstStrategy.java
@Component("bottleneckFirst")

Drum-Buffer-Rope (DBR) algorithm:
1. Identify bottleneck resource (most loaded or explicitly marked)
2. Schedule bottleneck first (the 'Drum'):
   - Sort bottleneck jobs by EDD
   - Group consecutive same-product jobs to minimize changeover (PCBA key optimization)
   - Schedule on bottleneck resource timeline
3. Schedule non-bottleneck operations around bottleneck (the 'Rope'):
   - Pre-bottleneck ops: backward from bottleneck start
   - Post-bottleneck ops: forward from bottleneck end
4. Add time buffer before bottleneck to protect against upstream delays

Helper: identifyBottleneck(request) — finds resource with longest total processing time (or user-specified bottleneck via metadata)

PCBA optimization: when sorting bottleneck jobs, group by productId to minimize SMT changeover (changeover = 30min-2h in PCBA).

Test: platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/BottleneckFirstStrategyTest.java

TDD:
- testIdentifiesBottleneck: 2 SMT resources with different load → correct bottleneck identified
- testBottleneckScheduledFirst: bottleneck jobs scheduled before non-bottleneck
- testSameProductGrouped: 3 jobs (product A, B, A) → reordered to (A, A, B) on bottleneck to reduce changeover
- testNonBottleneckAroundBottleneck: pre-bottleneck op scheduled before, post-bottleneck after
- testBufferTimeAdded: configurable buffer (default 30 min) added before bottleneck start
acceptanceCriteria: BottleneckFirstStrategy implements SchedulingStrategy
Bottleneck identified by total load or explicit marking
Bottleneck jobs grouped by product to minimize changeover
Non-bottleneck operations scheduled around bottleneck
Buffer time added before bottleneck
BottleneckFirstStrategyTest has 5+ tests
Tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*BottleneckFirstStrategyTest'
userStories.notes: TDD required. This is the most complex strategy and the most valuable for PCBA. SMT is typically the bottleneck in PCBA production. The changeover grouping is critical — changing SMT feeder setup can take 30min-2h, so scheduling same products consecutively is a major optimization.

userStories.title: Implement GanttDataBuilder for visualization output
acceptanceCriteria: GanttDataBuilder.java exists in module/aps/visualization/
GanttData, GanttRow, GanttTask, GanttMilestone DTOs exist
Operations color-coded by operation type
Resource utilization included in output
cd platform && ./gradlew compileJava succeeds
userStories.notes: No TDD for this story — it's pure data transformation. The colors match PCBA operations (SMT=blue, REFLOW=orange, etc). GanttRow should have equals/hashCode based on resourceId for distinct() to work correctly.

userStories.title: Implement SchedulingEngine + ApsController REST API
acceptanceCriteria: SchedulingEngine.java exists with schedule() and scheduleWithGantt() methods
ApsController.java exists with POST /api/aps/schedule, POST /api/aps/gantt, GET /api/aps/strategies
Engine dispatches to correct strategy by name
Unknown strategy throws descriptive error
SchedulingEngineTest has 3+ tests
Tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*SchedulingEngineTest'
userStories.notes: TDD required. Spring auto-injects Map<String, SchedulingStrategy> from @Component-named beans. The buildScheduleRequest() in controller queries dynamic tables to load work orders, resources, and calendars — implement a simplified version that works with the request data directly.

userStories.title: Implement MES Handlers (ConfirmWorkOrder, StartOp, CompleteOp, ReportWork)
userStories.description:
Create 4 MES-related PF4J handlers for the PCBA ERP plugin.

All handlers in: plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/main/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/

1. ConfirmWorkOrderHandler.java — pe:confirm_work_order (already exists as STATE_TRANSITION on pe_production_plan)
   - When work order is confirmed, explode routing into work order operations
   - Logic:
     a. Get work order to find product_id: db.getById("pe_production_plan", recordId)
     b. Find active routing: db.query("pe_routing", Map.of("pe_rt_product_id", productId, "pe_rt_status", "ENABLED"))
     c. If no routing found, skip (no operations to create)
     d. Get operations: db.query("pe_operation", Map.of("pe_op_routing_id", routingId)) ORDER BY pe_op_seq
     e. For each operation, create pe_work_order_op record with: woo_work_order_id, woo_operation_id, woo_seq, woo_name, woo_status=PENDING, woo_planned_qty=work order qty
     f. Return opsCreated count

2. StartOperationHandler.java — pe:start_operation
   - Records actual start time on the work order operation
   - Logic: update pe_woo_actual_start = now(), status change handled by platform STATE_TRANSITION

3. CompleteOperationHandler.java — pe:complete_operation  
   - Records actual end time, calculates yield rate
   - Logic: update pe_woo_actual_end = now(), calculate yield_rate = (actual_qty - defect_qty) / actual_qty * 100
   - If this is the last operation (all ops COMPLETED/SKIPPED), update work order status

4. ReportWorkHandler.java — pe:report_work (triggered by pe:create_work_report)
   - When a work report is created, aggregate quantities to the parent work order operation
   - Logic:
     a. Get work report to find work_order_op_id
     b. Sum all work reports for this operation: SUM(qty_completed), SUM(qty_defect)
     c. Update pe_work_order_op: woo_actual_qty = sum(completed), woo_defect_qty = sum(defect)
     d. Recalculate yield_rate

Also add the pe:confirm_work_order command binding in commands.json if not already present:
- STATE_TRANSITION on pe_production_plan, stateField pe_pp_status, fromStates ["DRAFT"], toState "CONFIRMED", handler "pe:confirm_work_order"

No separate unit tests for handlers (they use DataAccessor which requires mocking). Tests in US-T2.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmWorkOrderHandler explodes routing into work order operations
StartOperationHandler sets actual_start time
CompleteOperationHandler sets actual_end and calculates yield_rate
ReportWorkHandler aggregates work report quantities to work order operation
All 4 handlers have @Extension and implement CommandHandlerExtension
pe:confirm_work_order command exists in commands.json if missing
cd plugins/pcba-erp/backend && gradle compileJava succeeds
userStories.notes: Follow existing handler patterns (ConfirmWarehouseInHandler etc). Use DataAccessor for all DB operations. The routing→operations explosion is the key feature — it transforms a BOM-level work order into executable operation-level tasks. Check that pe_production_plan model and pe_pp_status field exist in the current plugin config.

userStories.title: Implement Quality Handlers (IQC + FQC creation and completion)
userStories.description:
Create 4 quality-related PF4J handlers.

All handlers in: plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/main/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/

1. CreateIqcOnReceiptHandler.java — triggers when warehouse in is confirmed (pe:confirm_warehouse_in)
   IMPORTANT: Modify existing ConfirmWarehouseInHandler.java to call IQC creation AFTER inventory update.
   OR create a separate handler that listens to a different command. 
   Simplest approach: Add IQC creation logic at the END of ConfirmWarehouseInHandler.execute().
   - For each WH In line, if the WH In type is PURCHASE_IN:
     a. Create pe_iqc_order record: auto-generate code "IQC-{date}-{seq}", set receipt_id, supplier_id (from PO if available), material_id, qty_received, result=PENDING
   - If type is not PURCHASE_IN (e.g. RETURN_IN), skip IQC creation

2. CompleteIqcHandler.java — pe:complete_iqc
   - Evaluates inspection results and sets the QC outcome
   - Logic:
     a. Get IQC order
     b. Compare qty_accepted vs qty_received: if accepted >= received → PASS, if accepted = 0 → FAIL, else CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT
     c. Calculate: qty_rejected = qty_inspected - qty_accepted (if not already set)
     d. Update pe_iqc_result based on comparison
     e. Return the result

3. CreateFqcOnCompleteHandler.java — triggers when all work order ops are completed
   Simplest approach: Add FQC creation at the END of CompleteOperationHandler.execute() when it detects all ops are done.
   - When the last operation is completed:
     a. Create pe_fqc_order: auto-generate code "FQC-{date}-{seq}", set work_order_id, product_id, batch_no (from work order if available), qty_inspected = work order qty, result=PENDING

4. CompleteFqcHandler.java — pe:complete_fqc
   - Similar to CompleteIqcHandler: evaluate pass/fail based on quantities
   - If PASS: update batch_trace status to QC_PASSED (if batch exists)
   - If FAIL: update batch_trace status to QC_FAILED

Note on auto-code generation: In handlers, generate codes manually ("IQC-" + dateFormat + "-" + random/seq) since AUTO_GENERATE strategy only works through Command pipeline CREATE operations.
acceptanceCriteria: IQC orders auto-created when PURCHASE_IN warehouse receipt is confirmed
CompleteIqcHandler evaluates PASS/FAIL/CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT based on quantities
FQC order auto-created when last work order operation is completed
CompleteFqcHandler evaluates and optionally updates batch_trace status
All handlers compile: cd plugins/pcba-erp/backend && gradle compileJava succeeds
userStories.notes: IQC/FQC auto-creation is event-driven (triggered by other handlers). The simplest approach is to add logic to existing handlers rather than implementing a full event bus (which comes in Run 5). For code generation, use SimpleDateFormat("yyyyMMdd") + UUID.randomUUID().toString().substring(0,4) for a simple unique code.

userStories.title: Unit tests for APS engine + MES/Quality handlers
userStories.description:
Write comprehensive unit tests for all APS and handler code.

Test files to create:

1. platform/src/test/java/com/auraboot/module/aps/engine/ApsComprehensiveTest.java
- Test all 4 strategies with same input → different scheduling order
- Test resource utilization calculation
- Test conflict detection across strategies
- Test setup time optimization in BottleneckFirst
- At least 10 tests

2. plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/test/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/MesHandlerTest.java
- testConfirmWorkOrderExplodesRouting: mock routing with 3 ops → 3 work order ops created
- testConfirmWorkOrderNoRouting: no routing → no ops created, no error
- testStartOperationSetsActualStart
- testCompleteOperationCalculatesYield: actual=100, defect=5 → yield=95%
- testReportWorkAggregatesQuantities: 3 reports → sum aggregated to parent op
- At least 8 tests

3. plugins/pcba-erp/backend/src/test/java/com/auraboot/plugins/pcbaerp/handler/QualityHandlerTest.java
- testIqcCreatedOnPurchaseReceipt
- testIqcNotCreatedOnReturnReceipt
- testCompleteIqcPass: accepted >= received → PASS
- testCompleteIqcFail: accepted = 0 → FAIL  
- testCompleteIqcConditionalAccept: 0 < accepted < received → CONDITIONAL_ACCEPT
- testFqcCreatedOnLastOpComplete
- At least 8 tests

Total: 26+ test methods across 3 files.
acceptanceCriteria: ApsComprehensiveTest.java has 10+ tests covering all 4 strategies
MesHandlerTest.java has 8+ tests covering all MES handlers
QualityHandlerTest.java has 8+ tests covering IQC/FQC handlers
All tests pass: cd platform && ./gradlew test --tests '*aps*' succeeds
All tests pass: cd plugins/pcba-erp/backend && gradle test succeeds
Total 26+ test methods across all test files
userStories.notes: Use Mockito for all port/adapter mocking. For handler tests, use the same MockDataAccessor pattern from the existing HandlerUnitTest.java in the plugin. For APS tests, create deterministic test fixtures with known job ordering.
